title: mappa coriacea
intro: Mappa coriacea, also known as the Indian Mulberry or Cow Itch Tree, is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant's roots, leaves, and bark have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Mappa coriacea include treating skin conditions such as eczema, wounds, and fever, as well as gastrointestinal issues like diarrhea and ulcers. Mappa coriacea can be prepared into herbal remedies like decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
